operating_state: Net sales increased 12% to $15.8 billion in Q3 2023 and 24% for nine months to $45.8 billion, driven by price realization across all segments and improved supply chain conditions enabling higher production volumes. Financial services revenue increased 47% in Q3 due to higher average receivable balances and elevated financing rates, reflecting robust demand and favorable pricing dynamics.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio improved to 67.4% in Q3 and 67.7% for nine months from prior year levels of 73.2% and 74.9% respectively, driven by price realization partially offset by elevated component, energy, and labor expenses. Operating expenses increased with technology investment, inflationary wage pressures and profit-sharing incentives. Interest expense more than doubled reflecting higher borrowing costs and increased debt levels.
material_events: Company executed $4.7 billion in share repurchases during nine months under its $18 billion authorization, reducing share count to 290.8 million from 304.1 million prior year. Board declared quarterly dividend of $1.35 per share and expanded to 12 directors. Completed sale of Russian financial services operations for $36 million net proceeds while Brazil Superior Court ruling allowed $243 million income tax provision reduction plus $47 million interest income.
macro_exposures: Financial services operations experienced $133 million after-tax unfavorable financing spread compression due to rising central bank policy rates impacting borrowing costs faster than portfolio yields. Foreign exchange translation provided $925 million positive impact in nine months versus $784 million negative prior year. Supply chain disruptions continue improving from 2022 levels though remaining disruptions and interest rate impacts persist.
forward_outlook: Management expects strong equipment demand to continue through 2023 with full order books and solid agricultural fundamentals supporting near-historical high farm net income. Large agricultural equipment sales volume expected higher than 2022 in North America while small agriculture and turf volume expected lower due to reduced consumer-oriented demand. Construction markets forecasted steady with infrastructure spending offsetting commercial real estate moderation.
uncertainty_profile: Standard warranty obligations total $2.6 billion with extended warranty premiums requiring future service delivery, while financing receivables portfolio remains exposed to credit risk though allowance decreased reflecting strong agricultural fundamentals. Various unresolved legal actions including product liability, employment, patent and antitrust matters with reasonably possible losses not expected to be material. Derivative agreements contain credit support provisions requiring $435 million cash collateral posted at quarter-end.
